channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Breaking geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: UN Chief Guterres "deeply alarmed" by Middle East violence escalation
item.description: UN Secretary-General Antonio Guterres called for an end to violence in the Middle East, expressing deep alarm at renewed escalation between Israel and Iran as diplomatic efforts continue.

item.title: Drones from Ukraine war straying into NATO territory, raising tensions
item.description: Russian and Ukrainian drones are veering off course and entering airspace of NATO countries like Lithuania and Romania, creating security concerns for nations not directly involved in the conflict.

item.title: Pope Leo XIV visits Barcelona, addresses Spain's cultural tensions
item.description: Pope Leo XIV navigated Spain's culture wars during his Barcelona visit, addressing Catalan language issues and calling for migrant rights in a historic parliamentary speech that received a 7-minute ovation.

item.title: Congo Ebola outbreak reaches 100+ deaths amid conflict zone challenges
item.description: The Ebola outbreak in eastern Congo has killed over 100 people out of 550 cases in less than a month, with medical response hampered by ongoing conflict in the region.

item.title: US Apache helicopter crashes near Strait of Hormuz, crew rescued
item.description: A US Army AH-64 Apache attack helicopter went down near the strategic Strait of Hormuz for unknown reasons. Both crew members were safely rescued, according to US officials.

item.title: China shares first far-side moon samples with Russian scientists
item.description: Beijing delivered lunar samples collected during the Chang'e-6 mission to Russian researchers, marking the first time foreign scientists have access to far-side moon material.

item.title: Russia conducts naval drills in Baltic Sea during NATO exercises
item.description: Russia's navy held military drills in the Baltic Sea practicing missile launches and bombing runs while NATO conducted major exercises nearby, highlighting ongoing tensions.

item.title: Satellite images suggest Israeli air base hit in Iranian missile strikes
item.description: Analysis of satellite imagery indicates possible damage to a hangar at Ramat David Air Force Base following Iran's recent missile attacks, despite Israeli claims of minimal impact.

item.title: US troops test German Helsing AI attack drones in Europe
item.description: German defense company Helsing armed US soldiers with HX-2 drones for exercises in Lithuania, where they successfully spotted and engaged targets in joint drills.

item.title: Car bombing kills man in Russian military housing district near Moscow
item.description: A vehicle explosion in a residential area for military personnel outside Moscow killed one person, with Ukrainian media reporting it targeted a Russian officer.

item.title: UK Sanctions Networks Financing West Bank Settler Violence
item.description: Britain announced coordinated sanctions with five international partners targeting networks involved in financing, enabling and carrying out settler violence in the occupied West Bank.

item.title: France Bans Israeli Finance Minister Smotrich Over Gaza Recolonisation Call
item.description: France barred entry to far-right Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich, condemning his calls for Palestinian resettlement and recolonisation of Gaza, as part of coordinated sanctions with five allied nations.

item.title: Bulgaria Halts All Military Aid to Ukraine
item.description: Bulgaria Defense Minister Dimitar Stoyanov announced the country will no longer send weapons to Ukraine, marking a significant shift in European support for Kyiv.

item.title: Finland Moves to Allow Nuclear Weapons Transport in Crisis Situations
item.description: Finland defense committee backed legal changes to permit nuclear weapons transport during crises, moving restrictions into criminal code while allowing allied nuclear arms transit.

item.title: China Exports Surge Past Forecasts on AI-Driven Demand
item.description: Chinese export growth accelerated in May, beating expectations on strong demand for chips, autos and high-tech goods fueling the global AI boom.

item.title: Philippines protests China's floating structure on disputed South China Sea shoal
item.description: Manila has protested Beijing's deployment of a floating structure with personnel on Scarborough Shoal, a contested feature in the South China Sea. The move escalates tensions in the strategically vital waterway.

item.title: Xi cements sway over North Korea as Kim names China top priority
item.description: Kim Jong Un feted Chinese President Xi Jinping with lavish celebrations in Pyongyang, calling ties with China a strategic priority. The visit underscores deepening alignment between Beijing and Pyongyang amid regional tensions.

item.title: Trump approval near record low as Americans expect higher gas prices
item.description: Only 22% of Americans approve of how President Trump is managing the cost of living, with most expecting higher gasoline prices amid Middle East tensions affecting global oil markets.

item.title: Investors see tipping point for bonds after Iran conflict shreds old playbooks
item.description: Bonds failed to shield investors during the Iran-Israel conflict, but fund managers say debt may reclaim its safe-haven role if inflation starts hitting growth, marking a potential shift in market dynamics.

item.title: Iran Confirms Two Military Personnel Killed in Israeli Strikes
item.description: Iranian state media reports two high-ranking air defense personnel killed in Israeli strikes on Monday, as diplomatic sources caution that a deal to end the US-Iran conflict within days is highly unlikely.

item.title: Israeli Ministers Call for Bombing Beirut Over Hezbollah
item.description: Senior Israeli ministers call for striking the Lebanese capital and detaining women in retaliation against Hezbollah, escalating rhetoric amid ongoing cross-border conflict.

item.title: Secret Camera Found in UK Office Where China Embassy Approved
item.description: A hidden camera was discovered in the Whitehall building where UK ministers approved a new Chinese embassy, raising security concerns at a sensitive time for UK-China relations.

item.title: EU to Discuss Deporting Afghan Refugees with Taliban
item.description: European countries are preparing to discuss deportation agreements with the Taliban government for Afghan refugees, a controversial move as the bloc faces migration pressures.

item.title: US blacklists Alibaba, Baidu, BYD as military-linked Chinese companies
item.description: The Pentagon added dozens of Chinese companies including tech giant Alibaba, EV maker BYD, and search engine Baidu to its list of firms allegedly aiding Beijing's military, a move Beijing condemned as discriminatory.

item.title: Xi Jinping wraps up rare North Korea visit with deeper cooperation pledge
item.description: Chinese President Xi Jinping completed his first visit to North Korea in seven years, establishing a deeper understanding and clearer path for development of ties between the two countries.
